headlines.headline: Meloni Meets Rubio as Iran War Strains Italy-US Ties
headlines.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni held talks with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio amid unusual strain between Rome and the Trump administration over the Iran conflict, highlighting diverging European and American approaches to the crisis.

headlines.headline: Trump Announces Withdrawal of 5,000 US Troops from Germany
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump announced he will withdraw 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany as a rift over the Iran war widens between Washington and NATO allies, raising questions about the legal and practical implications of such a move.

headlines.headline: US Suspects Nvidia Chips Smuggled to China via Thailand
headlines.summary: A firm linked to Thailand's national AI initiative is suspected of helping smuggle billions of dollars worth of servers containing advanced Nvidia chips to China, Bloomberg reported, citing sources familiar with the matter.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy Criticizes Russia's Victory Day Ceasefire Logic
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said Russia's limited ceasefire for World War Two commemorations exposes the "strange and inappropriate" logic of its leaders, as peace efforts remain stalled.

headlines.headline: Dollar Heads for Second Weekly Fall Despite US-Iran Clashes
headlines.summary: The dollar was set for its second straight weekly decline as investors remained cautiously optimistic about a diplomatic resolution to the Iran conflict, even after renewed hostilities near the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Iran Reviews US Ceasefire Proposal Amid Ongoing Hormuz Clashes
headlines.summary: Iran is reviewing a 14-point US peace proposal that would require Tehran to halt uranium enrichment for 12 years and hand over its 60% enriched uranium stockpile. The proposal comes as both sides exchanged fire in the Strait of Hormuz despite a fragile ceasefire.

headlines.headline: David Attenborough Turns 100, "Overwhelmed" by Global Tributes
headlines.summary: The legendary British wildlife broadcaster David Attenborough turned 100 on Friday, receiving worldwide accolades for his decades of work bringing nature documentaries to millions. Attenborough said he was "completely overwhelmed" by the outpouring of birthday wishes.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Could Sign Gripen Fighter Deal Within Months, Sweden Says
headlines.summary: Ukraine's purchase of Swedish Saab Gripen fighter jets could be signed within months, Sweden's defense minister said during a visit to Stockholm. The deal would bolster Ukraine's air capabilities as the war with Russia continues.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine-Sweden Gripen Fighter Deal Could Be Signed Within Months
perspectives.summary: Ukraine's defense minister says talks for Saab-made Gripen fighter jets are progressing well, with a potential deal signed within months during his visit to Stockholm.

headlines.headline: Nigeria begins court martial of 36 officers over alleged coup plot
headlines.summary: Nigeria's government faces mounting pressure as 36 senior military officers stand trial before a special court martial over an alleged 2025 coup plot, raising concerns about the country's worsening security situation.

headlines.headline: Turkish defense firms secure $8 billion in export deals at SAHA Expo
headlines.summary: Turkish defense companies signed nearly $8 billion in export contracts during the first three days of SAHA Expo, a major defense trade show, highlighting Turkey's growing role as a defense exporter.

perspectives.angle: Turkish Defense Industry Showcases New Systems at SAHA 2026
perspectives.summary: Turkish defense manufacturers presented new-generation systems at SAHA 2026 in Istanbul, highlighting Türkiye indigenous military production capabilities.

headlines.headline: Bahrain expels three MPs after they voted against royal citizenship decree
headlines.summary: Bahrain has revoked the parliamentary membership of three lawmakers just days after dozens had their nationalities stripped in connection with the Iran war context, amid a widening crackdown on dissent.

headlines.headline: Iran seizes oil tanker Ocean Koi in Gulf of Oman
headlines.summary: Iran's navy seized an oil tanker named Ocean Koi in the Gulf of Oman over an alleged attempt to disrupt Iran's oil exports, Iranian state media reported, adding to escalating tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: WHO documents over 3,000 attacks on Ukraine healthcare since war began
headlines.summary: The World Health Organization has registered more than 3,000 attacks on Ukraine's healthcare system since the start of the war, marking one of the highest numbers of attacks on healthcare in any conflict.

headlines.headline: UAE intercepts drone and missile attack as Iran war ceasefire frays
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates said it intercepted missiles and drones as tensions escalate despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations between Iran and the US. The UAE Ministry of Defense said its air defenses were "actively engaging" incoming threats.

headlines.headline: US trade court rules Trump 10% global tariff illegal
headlines.summary: A panel of federal judges on the Court of International Trade blocked President Trump's 10% tariffs on most US imports, ruling in a 2-1 decision that the law sidestepped a Supreme Court ruling that struck down blanket tariffs imposed under emergency powers.

perspectives.angle: US trade court rules Trump tariffs illegal, issues narrow block
perspectives.summary: The US trade court ruled against President Donald Trump's 10% global tariffs, finding they were not justified under a 1970s trade law, though the court only blocked the levy on two small businesses and Washington state.

headlines.headline: US jet fuel shipments could ease European shortages amid Iran war
headlines.summary: Aviation experts say European airlines could use US-made jet fuel to combat soaring prices and limited supply caused by the Iran war. The International Air Transport Association warned fuel shortages may emerge if the Middle East conflict continues.

headlines.headline: Secret document reveals Russia's plans to supply advanced weapons to Iran
headlines.summary: A classified document obtained by The Economist shows Russia offered to provide Iran with innovative weapons in large enough quantities to inflict significant casualties on American and allied forces, beyond the intelligence support Moscow is already believed to have provided.

headlines.headline: European countries emerge as NATO leaders as U.S. role recedes
headlines.summary: President Trump decision to leave NATO in the dark before launching strikes on Iran has inflamed tensions, pushing European leaders to consider a future where the U.S. no longer leads the alliance.

headlines.headline: Indonesia volcano eruption kills three hikers on remote island
headlines.summary: Mount Dukono erupted on Halmahera Island, killing three hikers including two Singaporeans. Rescuers evacuated 14 climbers while searching for others who defied safety restrictions.

headlines.headline: Islamic State-affiliated group kills 40 in Congo near Uganda border
headlines.summary: Allied Democratic Forces attacked villages in eastern Congo overnight, killing at least 40 and burning homes. The group pledged allegiance to IS in 2019 and has long operated in the border region.

headlines.headline: Lebanon civil defense member killed in Israeli strike amid ceasefire
headlines.summary: An Israeli strike in southern Lebanon killed a civil defense rescuer despite the ongoing truce. WHO has verified 152 attacks on healthcare in Lebanon since the war began, with 103 deaths.

headlines.headline: Chinese-owned oil tanker attacked near Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: A Chinese-owned oil products tanker was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, according to Chinese media, as the US launched and then suspended a plan to help stranded vessels transiting the vital waterway.

perspectives.angle: Chinese-Owned Oil Tanker Attacked Near Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: A Chinese-owned oil products tanker was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz, with the chief engineer reporting it remains unclear who fired on the vessel amid ongoing regional tensions.

headlines.headline: US carries out retaliatory strikes on Iran after Hormuz clash
headlines.summary: The US military conducted retaliatory strikes on Iranian targets it said were responsible for attacking American forces, calling the hostilities "unprovoked" amid renewed fighting in the Gulf region.

headlines.headline: China sees weakened US deterrence on Taiwan as Iran war drains weapons
headlines.summary: Chinese analysts say America"s ability to deter a war over Taiwan is weakened by the Iran conflict, giving Beijing leverage ahead of President Trump"s upcoming summit with Xi Jinping.

perspectives.angle: China Expands Influence Across Asia as Iran War Creates Energy Crisis
perspectives.summary: The Iran war has left China's neighbors appealing for energy help, handing Beijing the regional sway it has long sought. Countries across Asia are turning to China as fuel shortages bite.

headlines.headline: UAE sends $100 million to train new Gaza police force under Trump-backed plan
headlines.summary: The UAE has transferred $100 million to Trump's Board of Peace to fund training of a 27,000-strong Gaza police force composed of Jordanian and Egyptian-trained officers, marking a significant step in post-war governance planning.

headlines.headline: China's J-35 stealth fighters to give Pakistan edge over India in fifth-generation tech
headlines.summary: Pakistan is set to acquire Chinese J-35 stealth jets, potentially giving it a technological advantage over India which lacks fifth-generation fighters despite having a larger overall fleet.

headlines.headline: German cyber chief warns: 'We are not at war, but we're not at peace either'
headlines.summary: Germany's cyber warfare commander says allied nations face relentless digital attacks from Russia and other adversaries, with cyber intrusions increasingly targeting defense industries as European rearmament accelerates.

headlines.headline: US-Philippine Balikatan drills expand with more nations and advanced missiles
headlines.summary: American and Filipino forces wrapped up annual exercises featuring troops from five additional nations and deployed advanced systems including NMESIS and HIMARS, demonstrating deepening Indo-Pacific security cooperation.

headlines.headline: North Korea Deploys New Artillery Systems Capable of Striking Seoul
headlines.summary: Kim Jong Un inspected new long-range artillery with ranges exceeding 60km to be deployed at the South Korean border, bolstering ability to hit Seoul, state media reports.

perspectives.angle: North Korea to deploy artillery targeting Seoul, commission first destroyer
perspectives.summary: North Korea announced it will deploy new long-range artillery systems capable of striking Seoul this year and commission its first naval destroyer. Leader Kim Jong Un inspected weapons production as the country continues military expansion amid stalled denuclearization talks.

headlines.headline: $7 Billion in Well-Timed Oil Bets Before Iran War News Sparks Scrutiny
headlines.summary: Legal experts and lawmakers are calling for investigations into whether well-timed oil trades before the Iran war news were based on inside information or leaks.

perspectives.angle: Russia to Buy Foreign Currency as Iran War Oil Windfall Boosts Revenues
perspectives.summary: Moscow will purchase forex for the first time since the Ukraine war began, as Middle East conflict-driven oil prices have boosted Russian crude export revenues. The move signals economic resilience despite sanctions.

headlines.headline: IOC Lifts All Restrictions on Belarusian Athletes
headlines.summary: The International Olympic Committee removed all restrictions on Belarusian athletes, clearing them for international competitions including LA 2028 Olympic qualifiers.

headlines.headline: Vance and Rubio Take Center Stage as 2028 Republican Contenders
headlines.summary: The Vice President and Secretary of State offered contrasting styles this week, signaling early positioning for a potential post-Trump Republican future.

headlines.headline: UK Labour Suffers Heavy Losses as Reform UK Makes Gains
headlines.summary: Partial results from English local elections show large losses for Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Labour Party and gains for the hard-right Reform UK party.

perspectives.angle: UK Local Elections: Reform UK Surges as Labour Suffers Heavy Losses
perspectives.summary: Nigel Farage's Reform UK party made significant gains in English local elections, while Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Labour Party suffered substantial losses in traditional strongholds.

headlines.headline: British MPs to Visit China for First Time in Seven Years
headlines.summary: A cross-party delegation of UK lawmakers will visit China this month for the first time since 2019, signaling warming ties following Prime Minister Keir Starmer's January visit.

headlines.headline: Trump Announces Withdrawal of 5,000 US Troops from Germany
headlines.summary: President Trump said he will withdraw 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany as tensions with NATO allies widen over the Iran war, raising questions about the future of the transatlantic alliance.

perspectives.angle: US troops withdrawal from Germany raises NATO concerns
perspectives.summary: President Trump announced withdrawing 5,000 US troops from Germany as rifts over the Iran war widen with NATO allies. The move tests transatlantic security ties.

headlines.headline: China Export Growth Quickens on Iran War Stockpiling
headlines.summary: China's export growth likely accelerated in April as companies rushed to stockpile components amid fears the Iran war could push input costs higher, a Reuters poll shows.

headlines.headline: ASEAN Leaders Meet on Iran War Economic Fallout
headlines.summary: Southeast Asian leaders are meeting under pressure to mitigate the impact on their economies from the Iran war, with one minister saying it "should not have occurred in the first place."

headlines.headline: Toyota Forecasts 20% Profit Drop as Iran War Disrupts Supply Chains
headlines.summary: Toyota halved its quarterly profit and forecast a 20% annual decline as supply chain disruptions from the Iran war and rising costs weigh on the world's largest automaker.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Seeks Singapore's Help for Return of Seafarers From US-Seized Vessels
headlines.summary: Pakistan's foreign minister requested Singapore's assistance in repatriating Pakistani and Iranian seafarers aboard vessels seized by the US amid the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Israel Opposition Bloc Could Secure 61 Seats Under Three-Party Merger, Poll Shows
headlines.summary: A new poll suggests a merger between Bennett, Eisenkot, and Liberman's parties could form a 61-seat opposition bloc without relying on Arab parties, challenging Netanyahu's coalition.

headlines.headline: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of violating Victory Day ceasefire
headlines.summary: Russia and Ukraine traded accusations of violating a two-day ceasefire announced by Moscow to cover Soviet Victory Day celebrations. The fragile truce failed to hold as both sides reported attacks along the frontlines.

perspectives.angle: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of violating Victory Day ceasefire
perspectives.summary: Both sides reported hundreds of ceasefire violations as Russia celebrates its World War II victory with a muted parade. Moscow will have no military hardware on display for the first time in nearly two decades, while Ukraine continues drone strikes deep inside Russian territory.

headlines.headline: Paraguay reaffirms Taiwan ties as China pressures last South American ally
headlines.summary: Paraguay President Santiago Pena visited Taipei to reaffirm diplomatic support for Taiwan, one of only 12 countries maintaining formal relations. China has intensified efforts to draw Paraguay away, with lawmakers visiting Beijing amid internal debate over economic costs.

headlines.headline: Armenia distances from Russia as Pashinyan says "not an ally" on Ukraine
headlines.summary: Armenian PM Nikol Pashinyan clarified Armenia is not allied with Russia on Ukraine, confirming he will skip Moscow Victory Day parade. The Kremlin summoned Armenia's ambassador after Zelensky's visit to Yerevan, showing tensions as Armenia drifts toward EU.

perspectives.angle: Armenia Distances Itself from Russia as Kremlin Fumes Over Zelensky Visit
perspectives.summary: Armenian PM Pashinyan declared Armenia is not an ally of Russia on Ukraine, while Russia summoned Armenia's ambassador to protest Zelensky's visit to Yerevan, marking a significant shift in Caucasus geopolitics.

headlines.headline: Maersk warns energy crunch to persist even if Iran peace deal struck
headlines.summary: Shipping giant Maersk said the Iran war has pushed fuel costs up nearly $500 million a month, with the energy crisis set to continue even if a peace deal is reached. The warning came as oil supplies tighten further with depleted storage tanks meeting peak summer demand.

perspectives.angle: Maersk Warns Energy Crisis to Persist Even with Iran Peace Deal
perspectives.summary: Shipping giant Maersk reported fuel costs surged nearly $500 million monthly due to the Iran war, warning the energy crisis will continue even if a peace agreement is reached.

headlines.headline: US and Iran Exchange Fire as Fragile Ceasefire Shows Cracks
headlines.summary: US military carried out retaliatory strikes on Iranian targets after renewed hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz. The UAE reported intercepting drones and missiles as Trump insisted the ceasefire still holds.

perspectives.angle: US and Iran Exchange Fire in Strait of Hormuz, Testing Ceasefire
perspectives.summary: The US military said it carried out retaliatory strikes on Iran after intercepting Iranian attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, in the most serious test yet of their month-long ceasefire. Both sides said they did not want to escalate.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Vows No Immunity for Enemies After Beirut Strike
headlines.summary: Israel struck a Hezbollah commander in Beirut, its first strike on the capital since the ceasefire. Netanyahu declared there would be no immunity for militants threatening Israel.

perspectives.angle: Israel Strikes Beirut, Netanyahu Warns No Immunity for Militants
perspectives.summary: Israel carried out its first strike on Beirut's southern suburbs since the ceasefire, targeting a Hezbollah commander. Netanyahu declared there would be "no immunity" for Israel's enemies amid escalating regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Global Views of US Fall Below Russia Under Trump, Survey Finds
headlines.summary: An annual democracy study shows US global perceptions have deteriorated for a second year and now rank worse than Russia, as Trump’s policies strain the NATO alliance.

headlines.headline: Lula Visits Trump Seeking to Avert Trade Tariffs
headlines.summary: Brazilian President Lula met with Trump in Washington to discuss trade relations and tariffs. The two leaders skipped a joint press appearance despite signs of thaw in relations.

headlines.headline: Russia ramps up assassination campaign against opponents abroad, Western intelligence says
headlines.summary: Western intelligence officials report Russia has intensified targeted killings abroad since the 2022 Ukraine invasion, going after Russian activists and foreign supporters of Ukraine using recruited proxies. Plots have been disrupted in France, Germany, Lithuania, and Poland.

perspectives.angle: Russia Ramping Up Assassination Attempts Against Opponents in Europe
perspectives.summary: European intelligence officials warn that Russia is intensifying efforts to kill dissidents and opponents across Europe, marking a dangerous escalation in Moscow's overseas operations.

headlines.headline: US stocks fall from records as oil prices yo-yo amid Iran tensions
headlines.summary: US stocks dropped from their record highs while oil prices fluctuated sharply as tensions between the US and Iran continued to roil global markets.

headlines.headline: Hamas Operating Training Camps in Turkey for Attacks on Israel
headlines.summary: Kan 11 News reports Hamas is using Turkish shooting ranges and drone schools to train operatives for future conflicts with Israel, raising tensions between Israel and Turkey.

headlines.headline: China to Supply J-35 Stealth Jets to Pakistan, Edge Over India
headlines.summary: Chinese J-35 stealth fighter jets are set to give Pakistan an advantage over India in fifth-generation aircraft technology, as India lags in acquiring stealth capabilities.

headlines.headline: UAE Air Defenses Respond to Missile Threat as Regional Tensions Escalate
headlines.summary: UAE authorities activated air defenses in response to a missile threat amid ongoing US-Iran hostilities, urging residents to follow official channels for safety updates.

headlines.headline: Third Round of Lebanon-Israel Direct Talks Set for Next Week in Washington
headlines.summary: Lebanon and Israel will hold their third round of direct negotiations in Washington next week, mediated by the US State Department, continuing unprecedented diplomatic engagement.

headlines.headline: US-Iran resolution at UN faces Chinese and Russian vetoes
headlines.summary: The US urged countries to support a UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks on the Strait of Hormuz, but diplomats say China and Russia are likely to veto it, highlighting geopolitical divisions over the conflict.

perspectives.angle: US Iran Resolution at UN Faces Likely Vetoes from China and Russia
perspectives.summary: The US urged countries to support its UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks and mining of waterways. China and Russia are expected to veto the measure.

headlines.headline: Pussy Riot protests Russia return to Venice Biennale
headlines.summary: Dozens of activists from the anti-Kremlin art collective Pussy Riot demonstrated in Venice, waving Ukrainian flags and chanting against Putin to oppose Moscow presence at the Venice Biennale for the first time since the 2022 invasion.

perspectives.angle: Pussy Riot Protesters Swarm Russia's Venice Biennale Pavilion
perspectives.summary: Activists from Pussy Riot and FEMEN staged a protest against Russia's presence at the Venice Biennale, releasing colored smoke and swarming the pavilion in pink balaclavas.

headlines.headline: Brazil Lula reports progress after Trump White House meeting
headlines.summary: Brazilian President Lula reported progress in relations with the US after a three-hour meeting with Trump in Washington, seeking to avert new trade tariffs that had strained bilateral ties.

perspectives.angle: Brazil's Lula says Trump indicated no plans to invade Cuba during White House meeting
perspectives.summary: Brazilian President Lula da Silva reported that Trump conveyed he has no intention of ordering a US invasion of Cuba during their White House discussion. The statement comes amid heightened US-Cuba tensions.

headlines.headline: Serbia rejects local bid for Russian-owned NIS oil firm
headlines.summary: Serbias President Vucic dismissed a bid by a recently formed local company to purchase a majority stake in the Russian-owned NIS oil firm, citing lack of expertise amid ongoing talks with Hungarian MOL.

headlines.headline: Oil prices jump as US-Iran hostilities threaten ceasefire
headlines.summary: Oil prices rose more than 1% after renewed fighting between the US and Iran threatened a shaky ceasefire and dashed hopes for progress on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a key transit route.

perspectives.angle: Oil Prices Rebound as Markets Weigh Prospects of US-Iran Peace Deal
perspectives.summary: Crude prices rose over $1 after sharp losses in previous session, with investors optimistic about diplomatic progress to end the Middle East conflict and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Trump Says Iran Peace Deal Could Be a Week Away
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump said a peace deal with Iran could be reached within a week as Tehran reviews the latest US proposal. The US also paused its "Project Freedom" operations in the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: US and Iran Near Short-Term Peace Deal
perspectives.summary: The United States and Iran are edging toward a temporary agreement to halt their war, with Tehran reviewing a proposal that would stop the fighting but leave contentious issues unresolved.

perspectives.angle: Trump sees swift end to Iran war as Tehran reviews US peace proposal
perspectives.summary: Tehran is considering a U.S. peace proposal that would formally end the conflict while leaving unresolved key demands that Iran suspend its nuclear program and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump expressed optimism about a quick resolution.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Peace Negotiator Arrives in Miami for Talks with US Envoys
headlines.summary: Ukraine top negotiator Rustem Umerov arrived in Miami for meetings with US representatives as peace talks on ending the war with Russia have stalled in recent months.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Peace Negotiator Arrives in Miami for US Talks
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian peace negotiator Rustem Umerov arrived in Miami for meetings with US officials as peace talks to end the war with Russia have stalled in recent months.

headlines.headline: Dollar Firms as US-Iran Hostilities Flare, Yen Steadies on Intervention Risk
headlines.summary: The dollar strengthened against major currencies after renewed hostilities between the US and Iran, while the Japanese yen held steady following verbal intervention warnings from Tokyo.

headlines.headline: UK Court Convicts Two Men for Spying on Hong Kong Dissidents
headlines.summary: A UK court found two men guilty, including a British immigration officer, of conducting surveillance on Hong Kong pro-democracy activists in Britain on behalf of China, in an unprecedented shadow policing case.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah Claims 13 Attacks on Israeli Forces in Southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: Hezbollah announced it carried out drone and artillery strikes on Israeli troops, Merkava tanks, an Iron Dome platform, and military positions in southern Lebanon amid ongoing cross-border escalation.

headlines.headline: Turkey Unveils First Intercontinental Ballistic Missile Yildirimhan
headlines.summary: Turkey revealed its first ICBM with a 6,000km range and Mach 25 speed, representing a major advancement in Turkish defense capabilities and regional deterrence.

headlines.headline: US strikes Iranian military facilities after warship attacks in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: The US military targeted Iranian missile sites, command centers, and surveillance nodes after Iran fired on US Navy ships in the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump threatened further strikes if Iran does not accept a peace deal.

perspectives.angle: US Navy intercepts Iranian attacks on three warships in Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: The US military said it intercepted Iranian missile, drone, and small boat attacks targeting three Navy destroyers in the Strait of Hormuz, as tensions threaten the month-old ceasefire between Washington and Tehran.

headlines.headline: German finance minister blames Trump Iran war for economic slowdown
headlines.summary: Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil called Trump's "irresponsible war in Iran" a key factor harming Germany's economy, highlighting growing transatlantic tensions over the conflict.

perspectives.angle: US-Germany Rift Deepens Over Iran War as Trump Orders Troop Withdrawal
perspectives.summary: German Chancellor Merz criticized Trump for having "no strategy" on Iran, prompting the US to announce withdrawal of 5,000 troops from Germany. Berlin dispatched minesweepers to the Mediterranean as the diplomatic row escalates.

headlines.headline: China announces suspended death sentences for former defense ministers
headlines.summary: Two former Chinese defense ministers received suspended death sentences following a sweeping anti-corruption purge that has ousted several top military officials.

headlines.headline: Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
headlines.summary: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting amid an impasse in disarmament negotiations with Hamas, raising concerns about a new escalation.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait lift US military access restrictions
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait have lifted restrictions on US military use of their bases and airspace that were imposed after the launch of the US operation to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Wall Street Journal.

perspectives.angle: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait Lift US Military Access Restrictions
perspectives.summary: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait have lifted restrictions on US military use of their bases and airspace that were imposed after the launch of Operation to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the Wall Street Journal reports.

headlines.headline: Rubio approves .8 billion in weapons sales to Middle East partners
headlines.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio approved the sale of hundreds of air-defense interceptors and other weapons to Middle East partners in deals worth .8 billion as the region remains volatile.

headlines.headline: EU prepares for potential talks with Vladimir Putin
headlines.summary: European Union leaders are preparing for potential talks with Russian President Vladimir Putin, the Financial Times reported, citing a senior official.

headlines.headline: China asks banks to pause loans to US-sanctioned refineries
headlines.summary: China's financial regulator has advised the country's largest lenders to temporarily suspend new loans to five refineries recently sanctioned by the US over their ties to Iranian oil, Bloomberg News reported.

perspectives.angle: China Tells Banks to Halt Loans to US-Sanctioned Iranian Oil Refineries
perspectives.summary: Chinese regulators advised major lenders to suspend new loans to five refineries sanctioned by the US over ties to Iranian oil, signaling Beijing may be bowing to Washington pressure.

headlines.headline: Poland's president pushes referendum on EU climate policy amid political roadblocks
headlines.summary: Polish President Andrzej Duda is advocating for a referendum on EU climate policy, though the proposal faces opposition in the Senate. The debate highlights tensions over energy transition costs and sovereignty.

headlines.headline: China urged to forge 'spear' for cognitive warfare amid great power rivalry
headlines.summary: Prominent Chinese academics are calling on Beijing to move beyond a defensive mindset and actively shape international public opinion. The recommendations come as competition with Western powers intensifies.

headlines.headline: Japan signals unlimited yen defense, in daily contact with US
headlines.summary: Japans top currency diplomat said the country faces no constraints on currency intervention frequency and is in daily contact with US authorities, reinforcing Tokyos resolve to defend the embattled yen.

headlines.headline: Gold Fields flags cost pressures as Iran war drives up input prices
headlines.summary: South Africas Gold Fields expects costs to increase as the Iran war has driven prices of fuel and explosives higher, highlighting conflict impact on mining sector.

perspectives.angle: U.S. Gasoline Prices Top $4.50 Per Gallon Amid Iran War Disruptions
perspectives.summary: The U.S. national average retail gasoline price surpassed $4.50 a gallon for the first time since July 2022, as the U.S.-Iran conflict continues to disrupt oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. The spike comes ahead of the summer driving season.

headlines.headline: India-Pakistan ceasefire holds one year on, but little else does
headlines.summary: One year after the India-Pakistan conflict, the ceasefire continues but underlying tensions remain unresolved. The crisis was triggered by a militant attack on tourists in Kashmir.

headlines.headline: US not planning imminent military action in Cuba despite Trump threats
headlines.summary: US officials say the administration isnt looking at imminent military action against Havana despite Trumps threats, as new sanctions target Cuban military conglomerate.

headlines.headline: British voters cast ballots in elections expected to deal blow to Starmer
headlines.summary: Elections for almost 5,000 council seats in England and to the parliaments in Scotland and Wales could signal the beginning of the end of Britain traditional two-party system if voters opt for populist and nationalist parties.

headlines.headline: Baltic nations seek more NATO air defences after drones hit Latvian oil facility
headlines.summary: Latvia and Lithuania called on NATO to boost air defences after two suspected Ukrainian drones crossed from Russian territory and crashed in Latvia, one exploding at an oil storage facility.

headlines.headline: US imposes sanctions on Cuban military conglomerate and mining joint venture
headlines.summary: The Trump administration targeted sources of foreign investment for Cuba communist leaders by sanctioning a business conglomerate run by Cubas military and a Cuban-Canadian mining joint venture.

headlines.headline: Xi Positions for Stability as Iran War Casts Shadow Over Trump Summit
headlines.summary: As Xi Jinping prepares to host Trump, Beijing faces the challenge of managing China-Iran economic ties while seeking to preserve the US-China relationship. The Iran conflict threatens to dominate the Beijing summit agenda.

headlines.headline: Iran War Threatens Global Food Security, UN Warns
headlines.summary: The Hormuz blockade is cutting off vital agricultural inputs, with the UN FAO warning of harvest risks. China identified as key to maintaining food supply chains amid the crisis.

headlines.headline: CIA Assessment: Iran Retains 70% of Pre-War Missile Stockpile, Can Withstand Blockade for Months
headlines.summary: Intelligence report contradicts public statements from Trump officials, suggesting Iran has significant military capacity remaining and can endure the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

perspectives.angle: US Intelligence Estimates Iran Can Outlast Hormuz Blockade for Months
perspectives.summary: Iran retains about 75% of prewar inventories of mobile launchers and 70% of missile stockpiles, according to a Washington Post report. The assessment suggests Tehran has significant resilience despite ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump Gives EU Until July 4 to Ratify Trade Deal or Face Higher Tariffs
headlines.summary: President Trump set a two-month deadline for the European Union to implement the trade deal agreed last year, threatening significantly higher tariffs if not fulfilled by Independence Day.

headlines.headline: Rubio Meets Pope Leo to Mend US-Vatican Relations Amid Trump Tensions
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio held a private meeting with Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican, discussing religious freedom and humanitarian issues as tensions persist between Trump and the pontiff.

perspectives.angle: Pope Leo and Rubio Commit to Stronger US-Vatican Ties
perspectives.summary: Pope Leo and US Secretary of State Marco Rubio pledged to improve Vatican-Washington relations during talks in Rome, seeking to mend diplomatic tensions following Trump's repeated attacks on the pontiff over Iran policy.

headlines.headline: US Sells $17 Billion in Missiles to Gulf Nations as Stockpiles Deplete
headlines.summary: The US and partners have exhausted air defense missiles in the Iran war, and now Washington is selling more missiles worth $17 billion to Gulf allies as production struggles to keep pace.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Launches Major Drone Attack on Moscow
headlines.summary: Russia reported shooting down 347 Ukrainian drones overnight in a major attack after Moscow shunned a ceasefire offer, marking one of the largest drone assaults on the Russian capital.

headlines.headline: Dollar Faces Trapdoor if Iran War Ends
headlines.summary: Fresh optimism over a possible end to the Iran war has pushed the US dollar back to pre-conflict lows, with analysts warning the greenback could be a casualty of any peace deal.

headlines.headline: Japan Signals Unlimited Yen Defense
headlines.summary: Japan faces no constraints on currency market interventions and is in daily contact with US authorities, reinforcing Tokyo resolve to defend the embattled yen amid global volatility.

headlines.headline: What At Stake at Trump-Xi Summit
headlines.summary: President Trump is scheduled to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing next week, as both countries seek to stabilize a relationship strained by tensions over trade, Taiwan and the Iran war.

headlines.headline: US Sanctions Cuba Nickel Operation Targeting China-Linked Supply Chains
headlines.summary: Marco Rubio announced new sanctions on Thursday, escalating the Trump administration pressure campaign against Havana. The move targets nickel operations with implications for China-linked critical mineral supply chains.

headlines.headline: Paris Prosecutor Opens Judicial Investigation Into Elon Musk and X
headlines.summary: Paris public prosecutor has opened a formal judicial investigation into Elon Musk's X social media platform, marking a new step in an ongoing probe.

headlines.headline: UK Press Watchdog Rules Publications Can Call Gaza War a Genocide
headlines.summary: The Independent Press Standards Organisation has ruled that publications are entitled to describe Israel's actions in Gaza as genocide, a significant media ethics decision.

headlines.headline: French prosecutors seek charges against Elon Musk and X over abuse images and Grok deepfakes
headlines.summary: Paris prosecutors opened an investigation into X for complicity in distributing child sexual abuse images, deepfakes, and Holocaust denial by the AI system Grok. Musk and former X CEO Yaccarino were summoned for voluntary interviews but did not appear.

headlines.headline: Trump FEMA council proposes sweeping changes to US disaster aid system
headlines.summary: A council appointed by President Trump approved recommendations to overhaul FEMA, potentially reducing federal disaster support and shifting more responsibility to states. The changes would affect how disasters qualify for federal aid and how states receive reimbursement.

headlines.headline: EU Leaders Prepare for Potential Talks With Putin
headlines.summary: European Union leaders are preparing for potential talks with Russian President Vladimir Putin, according to a senior official cited by the Financial Times. The move signals a possible shift in diplomatic strategy as the Ukraine conflict continues.

headlines.headline: UK Elections Could Reshape British Politics
headlines.summary: Elections for nearly 5,000 council seats in England and parliaments in Scotland and Wales could signal the end of Britain's traditional two-party system if voters opt for populist and nationalist parties.

headlines.headline: US and China Weigh Launch of Official AI Discussions
headlines.summary: Washington and Beijing are weighing the launch of official discussions about artificial intelligence, the Wall Street Journal reports, as competition in the critical technology sector intensifies.

headlines.headline: Canada Dollar Outlook Bullish on Geopolitical Risk Easing
headlines.summary: The Canadian dollar is expected to strengthen if uncertainty linked to the Middle East war and US tariffs eases, according to a Reuters poll, as markets eye potential resolution of the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Satellite Images Show Iran Hit 228 US Military Targets, Far More Than Reported
headlines.summary: Analysis of satellite photos reveals damage to at least 228 structures at US bases across the Persian Gulf region. A new report estimates US spent $72 billion on the Iran war—nearly triple the Pentagon official estimate.

headlines.headline: Iran Reviews US Peace Proposal Conveyed Through Pakistan, No Final Decision Yet
headlines.summary: Iran confirmed it is reviewing a US ceasefire proposal transmitted via Pakistani mediation, described as a "14-point framework." Tehran has not yet responded to Washington as markets rally on hopes of Hormuz reopening.

headlines.headline: China Sentences Former Defense Ministers to Suspended Death
headlines.summary: China has announced suspended death sentences for former defense ministers Wei Fenghe and Li Shangfu following their ousting in a major military purge. The move highlights ongoing anti-corruption campaigns targeting top military brass.

perspectives.angle: Two former Chinese defense ministers sentenced to death with reprieve on corruption charges
perspectives.summary: Wei Fenghe and Li Shangfu, both former defense ministers, received suspended death sentences in a major anti-corruption purge. The sentences signal Xi Jinping's continued crackdown on military corruption.

headlines.headline: Three IS-Linked Women Arrested in Australia After Syria Return
headlines.summary: Three women allegedly linked to Islamic State have been arrested in Australia after flying home from Syria for the first time in years. The arrests highlight ongoing concerns about returning foreign fighters.

headlines.headline: Hungary Returns Seized $82 Million in Cash and Gold to Ukraine
headlines.summary: Hungary has returned a seized shipment worth $82 million in cash and gold to Ukraine, in a significant gesture after initially confiscating the assets. The move could signal improving Hungary-Ukraine relations.

headlines.headline: Latvia Calls for NATO Air Defense Boost After Drone Crashes
headlines.summary: Latvia and Lithuania urged NATO to strengthen regional air defenses after two suspected Ukrainian drones crossed from Russian territory and crashed in Latvia, with one hitting an oil storage facility.

headlines.headline: Kenya confirms 19 citizens killed fighting for Russia in Ukraine, 32 missing
headlines.summary: Kenya's foreign minister reported that 19 Kenyan nationals have died fighting on the Ukrainian frontline for Russian forces, with 32 others missing. This highlights the ongoing recruitment of foreign fighters by Russia in the Ukraine war.

headlines.headline: US sanctions Iraq's deputy oil minister for facilitating Iranian oil sales
headlines.summary: The US Treasury Department sanctioned Iraq's deputy oil minister Ali Maarij Al-Bahadly for helping Iran and its proxies divert oil for illicit sales. The sanctions target Iranian oil smuggling networks amid ongoing US-Iran tensions.

headlines.headline: Thailand and Cambodia pledge lasting peace at ASEAN meeting after border clashes
headlines.summary: Both nations agreed to build trust and pursue lasting peace following last year's border conflict, with further talks planned to solidify bilateral ties. The diplomatic breakthrough came at a regional ASEAN meeting.

perspectives.angle: Thailand cancels 25-year oil pact with Cambodia after border clashes
perspectives.summary: Thailand scrapped a 2001 agreement for joint offshore energy exploration with Cambodia, forcing Phnom Penh to pursue UN arbitration. The move follows armed conflict between the neighbors last year.

headlines.headline: China condemns US raid on Venezuela as hegemonic, vows continued support for Maduro
headlines.summary: China's Foreign Ministry reiterated opposition to what it called the US "hegemonic" raid on Venezuela and seizure of President Maduro, pledging continued support for Venezuela's sovereignty. The statement underscores China-US tensions over Latin America.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Tests Torpedo on Indigenous Submarine, Boosting Defense Against China
headlines.summary: Taiwan conducted its first torpedo test firing for a domestically-built submarine, marking a major milestone in efforts to strengthen deterrence against Chinese naval forces and protect critical sea lanes.

headlines.headline: US Submarines Maintain Edge But China Closing Gap
headlines.summary: The undersea domain remains America's last clear military advantage over China, but Beijing's submarine capabilities are rapidly improving as both nations expand their naval fleets.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Warns of Strong Response on India Clash Anniversary
headlines.summary: Pakistan's military warned of a strong response to any attack as it marks the anniversary of last year's conflict with India, heightening tensions between the nuclear-armed neighbors.

headlines.headline: IOC Urges Sports to Allow Belarus Athletes Without Neutral Vetting
headlines.summary: The International Olympic Committee says Belarusian athletes should compete with full national identity rather than being vetted for neutral status, marking a shift in post-Russia sanctions policy.

headlines.headline: Hungarian Voters Split on Ukraine EU Bid as Magyar Party Opposes Aid
headlines.summary: A new poll shows most voters of Hungarys opposition Tisza party oppose sending aid to Ukraine, while being divided on Kyivs EU accession bid, highlighting continued political tensions over Hungarys stance on the war.

headlines.headline: Israel Aerospace Industries Distances Itself From Turkey Critic Shay Gal
headlines.summary: Israeli defense company IAI stated that former employee Shay Gal no longer works there after he declared Turkey would become the next Iran, amid growing Israeli criticism of Turkeys regional stance.

headlines.headline: Gulf States Derail Trump Air Access by Cutting Airspace and Bases
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia and other Gulf states have suspended US access to airbases and airspace for operations against Iran, dealing a major blow to Washingtons military options and signaling regional reluctance to escalate.

headlines.headline: Global Debt Hits Record $353 Trillion as War and AI Drive Borrowing
headlines.summary: A new report shows China and the US driving global debt to unprecedented levels, with Iran war pressures, AI infrastructure spending, and energy security pushes expected to increase borrowing further.

headlines.headline: EU Cuts Funding for Green Projects Using Chinese Inverters, Drawing Beijing Rebuke
headlines.summary: The European Union has eliminated funding for renewable energy projects using Chinese inverters, prompting Chinas commerce ministry to warn the move would backfire and harm European energy security.

headlines.headline: Israeli Strike Kills Son of Hamas Chief Negotiator in Gaza
headlines.summary: An Israeli airstrike killed the son of Hamas' top negotiator as the militant group held talks in Cairo on safeguarding its truce with Israel, complicating US-mediated negotiations over Gaza's future.

perspectives.angle: Israeli airstrike kills son of Hamas chief negotiator in Gaza
perspectives.summary: An Israeli attack killed Azzam Al-Hayya, son of Khalil Al-Hayya, who leads Hamas negotiating team in US-mediated Gaza talks. It marks the fourth son of the Hamas leader to be killed in Israeli strikes, as negotiations continue in Cairo.

headlines.headline: US Senators Urge Stability and Cooperation in Beijing Visit Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
headlines.summary: A delegation of US senators visiting China called for stability and peaceful cooperation between the world's two largest economies one week before the Trump-Xi bilateral meeting.

perspectives.angle: China's Wang Yi Meets Iranian Foreign Minister Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
perspectives.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi met Iran's Abbas Araghchi in Beijing, calling for efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The meeting comes a week before Trump's scheduled visit to meet Xi Jinping.

headlines.headline: Singapore Oil Product Stocks Hit Over Nine-Month Lows as Iran War Cuts Supply
headlines.summary: Oil product stocks in Asia's trading hub Singapore fell to their lowest in more than nine months after the US-Iran war disrupted Middle East crude and fuel exports.

perspectives.angle: Shell Reports Strong Q1 Profits as Iran War Drives Oil Trading Gains
perspectives.summary: Shell beat profit estimates as the Iran conflict boosted oil prices and generated exceptional trading windfall, highlighting how geopolitical crises benefit major energy traders.

headlines.headline: Irish Footballers and Celebrities Urge Boycott of Israel Match
headlines.summary: Leading Irish footballers joined celebrities in a campaign urging Ireland to boycott a UEFA Nations League match against Israel, accusing the country of genocide in Gaza.

headlines.headline: Hong Kong Exchange Proposes Gold Futures as Geopolitical Volatility Drives Demand
headlines.summary: HKEX plans to relaunch gold futures as the city pushes to become an international gold trading hub amid bullion price volatility driven by Iran war tensions.

headlines.headline: Russian Drones Crash in Latvia, Damaging Oil Storage Facility
headlines.summary: Two drones from Russian territory entered NATO member Latvia and crashed, damaging an oil storage facility, in an escalation of spillover from the Ukraine conflict.

perspectives.angle: Two Russian Drones Crash in NATO Member Latvia, Damaging Oil Facility
perspectives.summary: Two drones entered Latvia from Russian territory and crashed, with one damaging an oil storage facility. The incident raises NATO concerns about spillover from the Ukraine war as Baltic states remain on high alert.

headlines.headline: China sentences two former defense ministers to death with reprieve for corruption
headlines.summary: Former defense ministers Wei Fenghe and Li Shangfu received suspended death sentences for graft, in the latest high-profile anti-corruption purge targeting China's military leadership.

headlines.headline: UK PM Starmer to seek even closer EU ties after reset deal
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces pressure from MPs to go further than the reset deal as he battles to salvage his premiership, signaling deeper UK-EU integration ahead.

headlines.headline: Turkey Togg partners with China CATL in first overseas EV chassis deal
headlines.summary: Turkish EV maker Togg and Chinese battery giant CATL will jointly develop vehicles based on CATLs Panshi EV platform, marking the first overseas deployment of the Chinese chassis technology.

headlines.headline: China May Attempt Taiwan Maneuvering at Trump-Xi Summit, Taiwan Warns
headlines.summary: Taiwan's intelligence chief warned that China may try to extract concessions on Taiwan when Trump visits Beijing next week, though the U.S. has reaffirmed its policy stance on the island has not changed.

perspectives.angle: China pushes Trump for concessions on Taiwan ahead of Beijing summit
perspectives.summary: China is pressing for US policy concessions on Taiwan as President Trump prepares to visit Beijing, seeking to capitalize on American preoccupation with the Iran war and stretched military resources.

headlines.headline: South Korea and Netherlands Agree to Expand Chips, AI Cooperation
headlines.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung and Dutch PM Rob Jetten agreed to deepen cooperation on semiconductors, AI, and quantum technology in their first phone call, strengthening ties between two key chip-producing nations.

headlines.headline: Moonshot AI valued at $20 billion as Kimi chatbot developer navigates China IPO rules
headlines.summary: The Chinese AI startup behind the popular Kimi chatbot raised $2 billion from Meituan and China Mobile, reaching $3.9 billion in total funding while adapting to Beijings new listing requirements.

headlines.headline: Israel Kills Hezbollah Commander in First Beirut Strike Since Ceasefire
headlines.summary: Israel carried out its first strike on Beirut since the ceasefire, targeting a Hezbollah Radwan Force commander. The attack raises pressure on the fragile truce that had halted Israeli attacks on the Lebanese capital.

perspectives.angle: Israel kills Hezbollah commander in first Beirut strike since ceasefire
perspectives.summary: Israel announced it killed a commander of Hezbollah's elite Radwan force in an airstrike on Beirut, marking the first Israeli strike on the Lebanese capital since the ceasefire agreement. The operation signals continued Israeli operations against Hezbollah leadership.

headlines.headline: US Fuel Stock Drawdowns Undermine Trump's Oil Price Promises
headlines.summary: Sharp drawdowns in US crude, gasoline and diesel stocks are flashing warning signs, undermining Trump's claims that prices will "drop like a rock" once the Iran conflict ends. Markets remain distorted with reserves at critical levels.

headlines.headline: US-China Consider Launching Official AI Dialogue
headlines.summary: Washington and Beijing are weighing the launch of formal discussions on artificial intelligence, the Wall Street Journal reported, amid broader efforts to stabilize US-China relations ahead of the Trump-Xi summit.

headlines.headline: Asia Faces Plastics Shortage as Middle East War Disrupts Oil Supply
headlines.summary: The Iran conflict has triggered plastics shortages across Asia due to oil and gas supply disruptions, affecting medical supply chains and manufacturing.

headlines.headline: TikTok Invests Record $25 Billion in Thailand Digital Infrastructure
headlines.summary: Chinese firm ByteDance makes massive investment to expand data centers and storage in Thailand, deepening digital infrastructure ties in Southeast Asia.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo to Visit Canary Islands on Spain Trip
headlines.summary: Pope Leo will visit two of the Canary Islands, major entry points for migrants braving dangerous Atlantic waters to reach Europe, as part of a tour of Spain next month.

perspectives.angle: Spain readies evacuations as hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for Canary Islands
perspectives.summary: Spanish authorities prepare to receive over 140 passengers and crew from a cruise ship where at least three passengers have died. US and UK arranging repatriation flights for their citizens.

perspectives.angle: UK Advises Hantavirus Cruise Ship Returnees to Self-Isolate
perspectives.summary: British health authorities advised two people who returned from the hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius to self-isolate. The ship departed for Spain's Canary Islands after three evacuations.

headlines.headline: Trump Says Iran Wants to Make a Deal
headlines.summary: US President Donald Trump stated that Iran wants to negotiate and make a deal, adding that the US was doing very well in Iran and operations were going smoothly. Tehran is reportedly reviewing a US peace proposal.

perspectives.angle: US-Iran Deal Framework Emerging After Trump Pauses Hormuz Operation
perspectives.summary: The US and Iran are moving toward a negotiation framework after Trump halted a maritime escort mission in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran is reviewing a US proposal while key sticking points over uranium enrichment remain unresolved.

headlines.headline: What's at Stake at the Trump-Xi Summit
headlines.summary: President Trump is scheduled to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping during his visit to Beijing next week, as both countries seek to stabilize a relationship strained by tensions over trade, Taiwan, and the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Equinor Sees Surge in Asia-Pacific Demand for Norwegian Energy as Iran War Disrupts Gulf Exports
headlines.summary: Norway's state energy giant reports increased interest from customers as far as Australia for petroleum and LNG exports, as Middle East conflict cuts off traditional Gulf supply routes.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan rejects emergency LNG bids as energy crisis deepens amid Iran war
perspectives.summary: Pakistan rejected all bids in an emergency LNG tender after surging spot prices made replacement fuel supplies too expensive, highlighting how the Middle East conflict is exacerbating energy shortages in import-dependent nations.

headlines.headline: Gold Rises for Third Day on Hopes for US-Iran Peace Agreement
headlines.summary: Gold prices extended gains as a softer dollar and optimism about diplomatic efforts to end the Middle East conflict supported the precious metal.

headlines.headline: EU Suspends Methane Rules as Energy Crunch Intensifies
headlines.summary: The European Union is delaying enforcement of methane reporting penalties as it prioritizes energy security during a worsening supply crunch caused by the Iran war blockade.

headlines.headline: Kazakh President Calls for Military Reform on Defender Day
headlines.summary: President Tokayev congratulated citizens on Defender of the Fatherland Day, reaffirming commitment to strengthen national defense amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: BAE Systems confident on growth as Iran war drives defense orders
headlines.summary: Britain's largest defense contractor says it's on track for 9-11% earnings growth this year as the Iran conflict keeps orders flowing, highlighting how geopolitical tensions are boosting Western defense industries.

headlines.headline: Paraguay president visits Taiwan as China ramps up diplomatic pressure
headlines.summary: President Santiago Peña arrived in Taiwan leading a business delegation, reaffirming Paraguay's diplomatic ties as one of only 12 countries worldwide that still recognize Taiwan. China has been intensifying efforts to isolate the island diplomatically.

headlines.headline: US Intelligence Links Trump Dinner Shooting Suspect to Iran War Motive
headlines.summary: A DHS preliminary assessment found the suspect had multiple grievances, with the Iran conflict potentially a motivating factor. The report dated April 27 was leaked as the war continues to create domestic security concerns.

perspectives.angle: US Intelligence: Iran Conflict Motivated Trump Assassination Attempt
perspectives.summary: DHS identified the US-Israel war with Iran as potential motive for the man accused of attempting to assassinate President Trump at a White House reporters gala last month.

headlines.headline: Russia Warns Foreign Diplomats to Evacuate Kyiv Ahead of Victory Day
headlines.summary: Moscow warned diplomatic missions to evacuate staff from Kyiv in case of retaliatory strikes if Ukraine disrupts May 9 Victory Day commemorations. The threat escalates tensions ahead of the symbolic Russian holiday.

perspectives.angle: Russia Warns Diplomats to Evacuate Kyiv Before May 9
perspectives.summary: Russia warned foreign diplomatic missions to evacuate staff from Kyiv if Moscow launches mass strikes in response to Ukrainian attempts to disrupt Victory Day commemorations.

headlines.headline: ASEAN leaders gather in Philippines amid Middle East energy crisis
headlines.summary: ASEAN summit in Cebu focuses on energy security and Middle East tensions, with fuel import-dependent Southeast Asian economies facing significant challenges from the Iran conflict.

perspectives.angle: ASEAN Summit addresses energy crisis from Iran war as 1,500 ships trapped in Hormuz
perspectives.summary: Southeast Asian leaders meeting in Cebu, Philippines are coordinating a regional response to the Middle East crisis, pushing for energy security and a negotiated settlement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The region faces significant risks as a major oil importer with nearly 70 days of Hormuz blockade.

headlines.headline: Australia forces gas giants to reserve 20% for domestic market
headlines.summary: Australia will require LNG exporters to reserve 20% of their gas for the domestic east coast market to avert supply shortfalls and lower energy bills amid global energy disruptions from the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: China condemns Eswatini for being "kept and fed" by Taiwan
headlines.summary: Beijing used unusually strong language against Taiwan's remaining African ally, saying Eswatini's leaders are being "kept and fed" by Taipei as diplomatic tensions rise.

headlines.headline: Zelensky announces legislation to legalize private military companies in Ukraine
headlines.summary: Ukraine is preparing laws to establish private military companies, creating postwar opportunities for veterans and expanding Kyiv's role in the global security sector.

headlines.headline: US Massing Troops Near Taiwan to Deter Chinese Aggression
headlines.summary: America is building up military forces near Taiwan as a deterrent against potential Chinese adventurism during the Iran crisis. The deployment signals US commitment to the Indo-Pacific despite Middle East demands.

headlines.headline: Russian Drone Attacks Spill Into NATO Territory in Romania
headlines.summary: Four years of war in Ukraine is taking a toll on neighboring Romania as Russian drones repeatedly cross into NATO territory. Residents live with the constant threat of weapons of war entering their airspace.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine war spills into NATO territory as drones hit Romania
perspectives.summary: Four years of battle in Ukraine is taking a toll on neighboring Romanians, with drones from the conflict crossing into NATO territory even as the war itself has not crossed the border.

headlines.headline: US Private Payrolls Post Largest Gain in 15 Months Despite Middle East Uncertainty
headlines.summary: US private payrolls rose significantly in April, pointing to continued labor market stability even as the Iran conflict clouds the economic outlook. The data suggests resilience in the American economy.

headlines.headline: US fires on Iranian oil tanker amid war pressure
headlines.summary: US military forces fired on an Iranian oil tanker as the Trump administration continues to pressure Tehran for a deal to end the ongoing conflict.

perspectives.angle: US Navy Disables Iranian Oil Tanker in Gulf of Oman
perspectives.summary: US military disabled an Iranian-flagged oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman on Wednesday for allegedly violating the blockade of Iranian ports, marking continued tensions even as diplomatic talks progress.

headlines.headline: Japan's Trump Supporters Lose Faith Amid Iran War Chaos
headlines.summary: Longtime Japanese supporters of Trump are questioning their allegiance as Iran war disruptions and troop withdrawal plans raise concerns about US-Japan security ties.

headlines.headline: India Sidelined by Iran War as Regional Diplomacy Unravels
headlines.summary: The Iran-Israel conflict has undermined New Delhi's delicate balancing act in the Middle East, leaving India without leverage as major powers reshape regional dynamics.

headlines.headline: Taiwan's Silicon Shield Exposed by Hormuz Crisis
headlines.summary: The Strait of Hormuz disruption reveals vulnerabilities in Taiwan's economic power, as its technology sector depends on stable global supply chains now under threat.

headlines.headline: Russian Commander Claims Western Funding for Mali Militants
headlines.summary: A Russian Africa Corps helicopter commander claims illegal armed groups attempting a coup in Mali receive Western funding, amid ongoing instability in the Sahel.

headlines.headline: North Korea declares it is not bound by nuclear non-proliferation treaty
headlines.summary: North Korea stated it is not bound by any treaty on non-proliferation of nuclear weapons, as state media KCNA reported Pyongyang continues to reject international pressure and sanctions to dismantle its nuclear program.

headlines.headline: North Korea revises constitution to drop reunification references
headlines.summary: North Korea has revised its constitution to define its territory as bordering South Korea and remove references to reunification, codifying leader Kim Jong Un's push to treat the two Koreas as separate states.

perspectives.angle: North Korea Drops Reunification Goal in Constitutional Shift
perspectives.summary: Pyongyang has removed references to "national reunification" and redefined inter-Korean borders in its revised constitution, signaling a potential move toward peaceful co-existence with South Korea.

headlines.headline: Ted Turner, CNN Founder and Media Pioneer, Dies at 87
headlines.summary: The billionaire media mogul who revolutionized cable news with the launch of CNN in 1980 has died. Turner also owned the Atlanta Braves and was a champion yachtsman, leaving an indelible mark on both media and sports.

perspectives.angle: CNN Founder Ted Turner Dies at 87
perspectives.summary: Ted Turner, the brash media pioneer who founded CNN and revolutionized 24-hour news broadcasting, has died at age 87. His vision transformed both the television industry and global news consumption.

headlines.headline: France Deploys Aircraft Carrier Toward Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: France moved its Charles de Gaulle carrier strike group to the Red Sea in preparation for a potential mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz. The move comes as Washington and Tehran negotiate a possible peace deal.

headlines.headline: Japan Nikkei Hits Record High Above 62,000 on Middle East Peace Hopes
headlines.summary: Japans benchmark Nikkei 225 surged past 62,000 for the first time ever, driven by strong tech earnings and growing optimism about a potential US-Iran peace deal. Asian stocks broadly rallied on the news.

headlines.headline: Venezuela Rejects ICJ Jurisdiction in Guyana Oil Territory Dispute
headlines.summary: Venezuela told the UN International Court of Justice it has no jurisdiction over its century-old dispute with Guyana concerning the oil-rich Essequibo region. Venezuela claims the territory was fraudulently taken in the colonial era.

headlines.headline: ECB Rate Hike Chances Rise Amid Inflation Pressures
headlines.summary: European Central Bank board member Piero Cipollone said the probability of an ECB rate hike has increased as inflation pressures remain elevated. The Iran war has complicated the global easing cycle.

headlines.headline: US Counterterrorism Strategy Adds Offensive Cyber Operations
headlines.summary: The Trump administration unveiled a new counterterrorism strategy that includes offensive cyber operations against groups deemed threats to US interests, marking a significant expansion of American counterterrorism tools.

headlines.headline: Pentagon AI Adoption Gives Cyber Criminals Nation-State Powers
headlines.summary: Pentagon leaders praise agentic AI tools that compress weeks of work into hours, but security experts warn the same tools are giving cyber criminals capabilities previously reserved for nation-states.

headlines.headline: Trump Counterterrorism Strategy Brands Europe Terrorism Incubator
headlines.summary: The new US counterterrorism strategy accuses Europe of fostering terrorism through mass migration while expanding domestic focus beyond Islamic extremism to include other threats.

headlines.headline: Japan Nikkei Surges Past 61,000 on Iran Deal Optimism
headlines.summary: Japanese stocks hit a record high as markets reopened after holidays, with optimism growing around a potential US-Iran agreement to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: G7 trade ministers discuss critical minerals as US-EU tariff tensions strain unity
headlines.summary: Trade ministers from the Group of Seven sought common ground on securing critical mineral supplies dominated by China, but fresh US tariff threats against European cars risked straining alliance unity.

perspectives.angle: Brazils Lula meets Trump at White House amid trade tariff tensions
perspectives.summary: Brazilian President Lula da Silva held talks with Donald Trump at the White House to reset relations strained by tariff disputes. Trump described the meeting as "very well" while Brazilian officials seek to avoid new trade levies.

headlines.headline: Japan fires missile in joint drill with US allies in Philippines near South China Sea
headlines.summary: Japan's Self-Defense Forces fired an anti-ship missile during exercises with US, Australian and Philippine forces, demonstrating growing military cooperation in waters facing the contested South China Sea.

perspectives.angle: Japan Fires Missiles During Military Drills, Drawing China Rebuke
perspectives.summary: Japan fired surface-to-ship missiles and sank an old warship in waters between the Philippines and Taiwan during major military exercises with US forces, prompting sharp criticism from Beijing.

headlines.headline: Gulf Economies Face Long-Term Hit from Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: The ongoing Iran-US-Israel war is dealing a sustained blow to Gulf economies, with Iranian strikes on gas fields and shipping disruptions threatening the region economic transformation plans.

perspectives.angle: Gulf states face uncertainty as Iran war reshapes regional alliances
perspectives.summary: Middle East expert warns that Gulf monarchies will have mixed feelings about the US after the Iran war, as the conflict exposes growing rift between UAE and Saudi Arabia over energy quotas and regional security.

headlines.headline: Fed Officials Warn Supply Chain Risks Fuel Inflation Concerns
headlines.summary: Federal Reserve officials said the US-backed war with Iran is raising the risk of sustained inflation shock, with high oil prices and growing concerns about global supply chain disruptions.

headlines.headline: A Year Into Papacy, Pope Leo Finds His Voice on World Stage
headlines.summary: Pope Leo marks his first year leading the Catholic Church with a higher public profile, having grown more outspoken on global affairs and drawn the ire of US President Donald Trump.

perspectives.angle: Pope Leo Marks First Year with Growing Global Profile
perspectives.summary: Pope Leo marks his first year leading the Catholic Church with higher public profile and outspoken stance on world affairs, drawing criticism from Trump over nuclear weapons position.

headlines.headline: North Korea declares it is not bound by nuclear non-proliferation treaty
headlines.summary: Pyongyang says the US and other countries are "tarnishing the atmosphere" at the UN NPT review conference by raising concerns about its nuclear weapons program.

headlines.headline: Israeli forces launch new incursion into Syrias Daraa province
headlines.summary: Israeli forces carried out an incursion in the Yarmouk Basin region in western Daraa countryside late Wednesday, marking continued Israeli operations in southern Syria.

headlines.headline: Russias YJ-20 hypersonic missile threatens US carrier dominance
headlines.summary: SCMP analyzes Chinas YJ-20 hypersonic sea-based missile, a highly maneuverable weapon that could sink aircraft carriers with a single hit and is nearly impossible to intercept.

headlines.headline: UAE Rejects Iran Criticism of US Defense Partnerships
headlines.summary: The UAE stated its international defense partnerships are a sovereign matter, dismissing Iranian claims that Abu Dhabi cooperation with the US threatens Iranian security.

headlines.headline: Australia Commits $7 Billion to Boost Fuel Stockpiles Amid Supply Concerns
headlines.summary: Australia will expand onshore fuel reserves to at least 50 days of supply, creating a government reserve of 1 billion liters to shield against future supply shocks following Middle East disruptions.

headlines.headline: China Enables Iran's Shahed Drone Supply Lines, Nikkei Analysis Finds
headlines.summary: A Nikkei Asia analysis examines how China facilitates Iran's Shahed drone production, which has been used extensively by Russia in Ukraine and by Iranian-backed groups across the Middle East.

headlines.headline: Hantavirus cruise ship heads to Canary Islands amid health emergency
headlines.summary: A luxury cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak departed for Spains Canary Islands after 3 passengers were evacuated. Four people have died from the outbreak, with at least 8 confirmed infections aboard the Dutch-flagged MV Hondius.

perspectives.angle: WHO Reassures Public on Hantavirus After Cruise Ship Outbreak
perspectives.summary: The World Health Organization stated the risk to the general public remains "absolutely low" after a hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship killed three passengers. A flight attendant who had contact with an infected passenger tested negative, easing concerns about person-to-person transmission.

perspectives.angle: Spain Prepares for Hantavirus-Stricken Cruise Ship Arrival
perspectives.summary: Spanish authorities are preparing to receive over 140 passengers and crew from the MV Hondius cruise ship, which is headed for Tenerife after a deadly hantavirus outbreak. The US and UK are arranging evacuation flights for their citizens aboard the vessel.

headlines.headline: France Deploys Aircraft Carrier to Red Sea for Potential Hormuz Mission
headlines.summary: France moved its carrier strike group to the Red Sea as part of planning for a possible mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz, urging Washington and Tehran to consider a diplomatic proposal given the global economic impact of their competing blockades.

headlines.headline: Euro Zone Services Activity Contracts for First Time in Nearly a Year
headlines.summary: Euro zone services activity slumped in April, contracting for the first time in almost a year as weakening demand and the Middle East war weighed on consumer-facing sectors. The deterioration adds to concerns about Europe economic resilience.

headlines.headline: Germany Weighs Bigger Stake to Block Italian Takeover of Commerzbank
headlines.summary: German government members are examining a drastic step to take a bigger stake in Commerzbank to fend off UniCredit unwanted bid, viewing the lender as strategic. The move reflects growing European banking tensions.

headlines.headline: Modi BJP Seizes Historic Control of West Bengal, Extending Grip to 70% of Indian States
headlines.summary: Narendra Modi ruling coalition now controls roughly 70% of India state legislatures after BJP stunning conquest of West Bengal, ending three consecutive terms of Mamata Banerjee Trinamool Congress. The victory represents the conquest of the party final frontier.

headlines.headline: Central Banks Pause Rate Cuts as Iran War Roils Markets
headlines.summary: The Iran conflict has forced major central banks to hold interest rates steady in April, disrupting global monetary easing efforts amid inflation pressures and market volatility.

perspectives.angle: Iran war pauses global central bank easing push
perspectives.summary: Major central banks have put interest rate cuts on hold as the Iran war creates inflation pressures and market volatility. The conflict has disrupted global energy markets and complicated monetary policy decisions worldwide.

perspectives.angle: South African Markets Rally on Iran War De-escalation Hopes
perspectives.summary: South African stocks, bonds, and rand strengthened after Trump signaled possible peace deal with Iran, boosting risk appetite for emerging market assets.

perspectives.angle: Barclays Bets No Fed Rate Cuts in 2026 as Iran War Fuels Inflation
perspectives.summary: Barclays joined a growing list of brokerages predicting no Federal Reserve rate cuts this year, citing prolonged high energy prices from the Iran war that are likely to keep inflation elevated.

headlines.headline: FBI Searches Virginia Senate Leader's Office in Corruption Probe
headlines.summary: The FBI executed a search warrant at Virginia Senate Leader Louise Lucas's office as part of an ongoing corruption investigation.

headlines.headline: U.S. NATO exit would force Europe into fiscal creativity and faster defense spending
headlines.summary: A Reuters commentary examines how a potential U.S. withdrawal from NATO would require Europe to spend more and faster than the $350 billion already pledged by 2035, suggesting low-cost tech adoption and new mutual financial schemes.

perspectives.angle: US NATO exit would force Europe into major defense spending surge
perspectives.summary: A potential US withdrawal from NATO would require European nations to spend more and faster than the $350 billion already pledged by 2035, according to a Reuters Breakingviews analysis.

headlines.headline: Poland warns Russia shifting from low-cost recruits to professional sabotage cells
headlines.summary: Poland raised alarms about Russia transitioning from using low-cost recruits to deploying professional sabotage cells, following what the prime minister called an unprecedented act of sabotage with explosions in November 2025.

headlines.headline: China Advances Submarine Detection Tech with Quantum SQUID Sensors
headlines.summary: Chinese scientists report progress on gravity-detecting SQUID sensors capable of identifying US nuclear submarines by their mass signature, potentially threatening US undersea supremacy.

headlines.headline: Eurovision Crisis Deepens Over Israel-Gaza Controversy
headlines.summary: Multiple countries are boycotting or threatening to withdraw from Eurovision 2026 over Israel participation, with artists including Massive Attack and Kneecap calling for fans to boycott the contest.

headlines.headline: Gulf's Trade Lifeline Hangs on Two UAE Ports Amid Hormuz Crisis
headlines.summary: The UAE ports of Fujairah and Khor Fakkan have become critical chokepoints for Gulf trade as the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked. Grain offloading and crude loading continue at Fujairah while regional supply chains reroute through the eastern UAE facilities.

perspectives.angle: Oman reports two injured as residential building in Bukha targeted amid Hormuz tensions
perspectives.summary: A residential building in Oman's Bukha region along the Strait of Hormuz coastline was targeted, injuring two people, as regional tensions escalate following US-Iran clashes in the strategic waterway.

headlines.headline: America massing troops near Taiwan to deter Chinese aggression
headlines.summary: The US is significantly increasing its military presence near Taiwan amid heightened tensions with Beijing. The buildup comes as China continues provocative actions in the Taiwan Strait.

headlines.headline: Putin is losing his grip on Russia
headlines.summary: An analysis suggests Vladimir Putin has led Russia into a strategic dead-end, with growing internal pressures and military setbacks in Ukraine undermining his authority. The war has exposed fundamental weaknesses in Russias military and economic model.

headlines.headline: Russia losing influence in Africa as setbacks mount in Mali
headlines.summary: Russian forces are facing significant setbacks in Mali, highlighting Moscows declining influence across Africa as local partners reassess their relationships.

headlines.headline: EU backs new compensation mechanism for victims of Russian war
headlines.summary: EU ambassadors approved a new compensation mechanism for victims of Russias war, though no country besides Ukraine has ratified the International Claims Commission.

perspectives.angle: EU Formally Backs Nuremberg-Style Tribunal to Prosecute Russian Leaders for War of Aggression
perspectives.summary: EU ministers voted to join a Council of Europe court that will prosecute Russian leadership for the crime of aggression against Ukraine, the same charge used against Nazi leaders at Nuremberg. The tribunal aims to hold Putin and other top officials accountable.

headlines.headline: Hungary returns seized Ukrainian bank assets in diplomatic breakthrough
headlines.summary: Hungary returned seized Ukrainian bank assets in what Zelensky called a civilized step, marking an improvement in bilateral relations.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia and UAE Drift Toward Open Rivalry Over Oil Policy and Regional Influence
headlines.summary: Competition between the Gulf powers has turned into tension over energy quotas, regional conflicts and differing visions for the Middle East, as the UAE leaves OPEC and Saudi Arabia asserts dominance.

perspectives.angle: Saudi-UAE Rift Deepens Over Energy Policy and Regional Conflicts
perspectives.summary: Tensions between Gulf allies mount over OPEC energy quotas and diverging approaches to regional conflicts, raising questions about Gulf unity.

perspectives.angle: Iran Demands Explanations From Saudi Arabia and UAE After Chinese-Made Drone Shot Down
perspectives.summary: Tehran seeks answers after a Chinese-manufactured drone was intercepted, raising questions about regional drone transfers and potential Gulf state involvement.

headlines.headline: Washington Post: Iran War Deepens US Political Divide Over Israel Alliance
headlines.summary: The Iran conflict has fractured the traditional bipartisan consensus on US-Israel ties, with both Democrats and Republicans now fiercely debating the alliance on campaign trails and in Congress.

headlines.headline: Bloomberg: UK Faces Disastrous Breakup as Four Nations Drift to Separatist Rule
headlines.summary: Polls suggest three of the four UK nations may soon be ruled by separatist parties dedicated to ending the union, a historic shift that could unravel centuries of political unity.

headlines.headline: Vietnam and India Seek Closer Ties to Hedge Against Superpower Dependence
headlines.summary: Both nations pursue deeper strategic partnership as they look to diversify away from reliance on major powers amid growing US-China rivalry in the Indo-Pacific.

headlines.headline: US dollar stuck in range as markets watch Strait of Hormuz developments
headlines.summary: FX strategists in a Reuters poll say developments in the US-Iran war will steer the dollar in the near term, with the currency expected to stay range-bound before weakening later this year.

headlines.headline: Syria Dismantles Hezbollah-Linked Assassination Cell
headlines.summary: Syrian authorities arrested a Hezbollah-linked cell accused of plotting to assassinate government officials. The operation signals Syria's continued efforts to assert control and address Iranian proxy operations on its soil.
